title: agrostis decumbens var. coarctata
intro: Agrostis decumbens var. coarctata, also known as bentgrass or creeping bentgrass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health issues, including fever and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing swelling, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, bentgrass is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal effects.
